/docs/ai/prompt-library.content:
# AI Prompt Library

## Drafting Prompt

```text
You are a senior bilingual travel editor.

Use only the supplied research brief and approved source notes.

Do not invent ticket prices, opening hours, transport schedules, accessibility claims, historical facts, or personal experience.

Write practical destination content that helps travelers decide whether to visit, how to plan, what limitations to expect, and what nearby destinations combine well.

Write Indonesian and English naturally and independently while preserving the same facts.

Mark missing facts with [VERIFY].
```

## Review Prompt

```text
Review this destination page for:
- Unsupported claims
- Missing verification dates
- Bilingual inconsistencies
- Unnatural translation
- Weak practical advice
- Missing internal links
- Missing source support
```

meta.title: AI Prompt Library
meta.description: Controlled prompts for research and drafting.

/docs/components/destination-library.content:
<EditorialNotice>
This is sample content for Phase 2 component integration only.
</EditorialNotice>

<Hero
  eyebrow="Bali"
  title="Example Destination"
  summary="A short example showing how destination MDX components are composed."
/>

<QuickFacts
  items={[
    { label: "Location", value: "Bali" },
    { label: "Duration", value: "2 hours" },
    { label: "Best time", value: "Morning" }
  ]}
/>

<TravelAdvisorScore
  items={[
    { label: "Scenic beauty", score: 5, explanation: "Strong landscape value." },
    { label: "Accessibility", score: 3, explanation: "Some uneven paths." }
  ]}
/>

<BestFor items={["First-time visitors", "Photographers"]} />

<AvoidIf items={["You require step-free access throughout"]} />

<TicketInformation
  entries={[
    { label: "Adult", price: "Needs verification" }
  ]}
  lastVerified="2026-07-16"
/>

<OpeningHours
  entries={[
    { dayLabel: "Daily", hours: "Needs verification" }
  ]}
  lastVerified="2026-07-16"
/>

<Facilities
  items={[
    { label: "Parking", available: true },
    { label: "Accessible toilet", available: false, note: "Verify on site." }
  ]}
/>

<Accessibility
  items={[
    { label: "Step-free entrance", status: "partial" },
    { label: "Uneven ground", status: "available", note: "Expect mixed surfaces." }
  ]}
/>

<TravelTips
  items={[
    "Arrive early.",
    "Bring water.",
    "Verify operational information before departure."
  ]}
/>

<NearbyDestinations
  items={[
    {
      name: "Related Destination",
      href: "/destinasi",
      distance: "10 km"
    }
  ]}
/>

<FAQ
  items={[
    {
      question: "How long should I stay?",
      answer: "Allow around two hours."
    }
  ]}
/>

<SourceList
  items={[
    {
      title: "Official Source",
      url: "https://example.com",
      accessedAt: "2026-07-16"
    }
  ]}
/>

<LastVerified
  date="2026-07-16"
  note="Recheck ticket prices and opening hours before publishing."
/>

meta.title: Destination Component Example
meta.description: Interactive Phase 2 reference showing every reusable destination MDX component.

/docs/destination/frontmatter.content:
# Frontmatter Reference

```yaml
---
id: tanah-lot
slug: tanah-lot
locale: id
name: Pura Tanah Lot
province: Bali
regency: Tabanan
category: temple
latitude: -8.6212
longitude: 115.0868
metaTitle: ""
metaDescription: ""
canonical: ""
lastUpdated: ""
lastVerified: ""
status: draft
---
```

Do not add new frontmatter fields without updating this reference first.

meta.title: Frontmatter Reference
meta.description: Canonical frontmatter fields for destination MDX.

/docs/destination/template.content:
# Canonical Destination Template

1. Frontmatter
2. Hero summary
3. Quick facts
4. Travel Advisor score
5. Best for
6. Avoid if
7. Overview — Indonesian
8. Overview — English
9. Highlights
10. Best time
11. Ticket information
12. Opening hours
13. How to get there
14. Suggested duration
15. Facilities
16. Accessibility
17. Travel tips
18. Nearby attractions
19. FAQ — Indonesian
20. FAQ — English
21. Sources
22. Legal photo references
23. Last verified
24. Editorial checklist

The template must remain consistent across all production batches.

meta.title: Canonical Destination Template
meta.description: Required section order for every destination.

/docs/developer/folder-structure.content:
# Folder Structure

```text
content/
  docs/
  destinations/
    id/
    en/

components/
  mdx/
  destination/

lib/
  content/
  seo/
  schema/
```

Documentation and destination content must remain separate.

/docs/developer/mdx-integration.content:
# MDX Integration

Requirements:

- Type-safe frontmatter
- Central component mapping
- Consistent heading anchors
- Table-of-contents generation
- Search indexing
- Safe code rendering
- No raw unsanitized HTML from content

Destination MDX components are implemented in Phase 2.

meta.title: MDX Integration
meta.description: Requirements for rendering documentation and destination content.

/docs/editorial/bilingual-guide.content:
# Bilingual Guide

Indonesian and English content must preserve the same facts while sounding natural in each language.

## Bahasa Indonesia

- Clear and practical
- Suitable for domestic travelers
- Explain unfamiliar terms
- Avoid stiff translation language

## English

- Natural international travel style
- Explain local cultural terms
- Do not mirror Indonesian sentence structure
- Keep facts and recommendations consistent

Do not translate line by line.

meta.title: Bilingual Guide
meta.description: Standards for Bahasa Indonesia and English content.

/docs/editorial/fact-check.content:
# Fact-check Guide

## Minimum Sources

1. Official operator or attraction
2. Government or official tourism authority
3. Independent operational source

## Must Be Rechecked Before Publication

- Ticket price
- Opening hours
- Access rules
- Temporary closures
- Transport schedule
- Parking
- Accessibility
- Facilities
- Performance schedule

Every changing value requires a `lastVerified` date.

/docs/editorial/writing-standard.content:
# Writing Standard

Every page must help a traveler answer:

- Is this destination worth visiting?
- Who is it suitable for?
- How much time is needed?
- What practical limitations should be expected?
- What should be combined with it?

## Rules

- Use original wording.
- Avoid unsupported superlatives.
- Explain trade-offs.
- Do not fabricate first-hand experience.
- Separate stable facts from changing information.
- Avoid padding content only to increase word count.

meta.title: Writing Standard
meta.description: Canonical editorial requirements for destination content.

/docs/getting-started/introduction.content:
# Introduction

Travel Advisor Java & Bali is a bilingual destination-content website focused on practical, trustworthy, and maintainable travel guides.

The project prioritizes content quality over platform complexity.

## Core Principles

- Write for travelers, not for keyword density.
- Never copy another publication.
- Never invent operational facts.
- Use at least three reliable sources.
- Mark changing information with a verification date.
- Write Indonesian and English naturally.

meta.title: Introduction
meta.description: Purpose and operating principles of the project.

/docs/getting-started/roadmap.content:
# Final Roadmap

## Phase 1 — Documentation Portal

Create the MDX documentation portal.

## Phase 2 — Component Library

Create reusable components for destination pages.

## Phase 3 — Golden Reference Batch

Create five benchmark Bali destinations.

## Phase 4 — Scalable Production

Produce all 161 destinations in consistent batches.

> This roadmap is locked. New phases must not be added without explicit project-owner approval.

/docs/glossary.content:
# Glossary

## Canonical Destination Template

The required section order used by every destination page.

## Dynamic Information

Information likely to change, including tickets, hours, access rules, and facilities.

## Golden Reference Batch

The five Bali destination pages used as the final quality benchmark.

## Last Verified

The date on which a changing fact was checked against a reliable source.

## MDX

Markdown extended with reusable UI components.

## Source of Truth

The canonical documentation that controls all production decisions.

meta.title: Glossary
meta.description: Canonical terms used across the project.

/docs.content:
# Travel Advisor Java & Bali Documentation

This portal defines how destination content is researched, written, reviewed, structured, and published.

## Project Focus

- Bilingual destination content
- Bali first, Java second
- Five destinations per production batch
- One consistent MDX structure
- Original writing
- Fact-checked dynamic information
- SEO-ready publication

## Final Roadmap

1. Documentation Portal
2. Component Library
3. Golden Reference Batch
4. Scalable Production

The project scope is locked to these four phases.

meta.title: Travel Advisor Documentation
meta.description: Single source of truth for the Java & Bali destination content project.

/docs/publishing/checklist.content:
# Publishing Checklist

## Editorial

- [ ] Original writing
- [ ] Indonesian reviewed
- [ ] English reviewed
- [ ] No unsupported superlatives
- [ ] No fabricated experience

## Fact-check

- [ ] Minimum three sources
- [ ] Ticket verified
- [ ] Opening hours verified
- [ ] Access rules verified
- [ ] Facilities reviewed
- [ ] Accessibility claims reviewed
- [ ] Last verified added

## SEO

- [ ] Unique title
- [ ] Unique meta description
- [ ] Canonical URL
- [ ] Alternate-language URL
- [ ] Internal links
- [ ] Schema validated

## Images

- [ ] Legal source
- [ ] License recorded
- [ ] Attribution added if required
- [ ] Alt text written

meta.title: Publishing Checklist
meta.description: Final quality gate before publishing a destination.

/docs/seo/internal-linking.content:
# Internal Linking

Every destination should include useful links to:

- Nearby destinations
- Same-area destinations
- Same-category destinations
- Suggested next destination
- Relevant itinerary page when available

Links must support trip planning, not exist only for SEO.

/docs/seo/metadata.content:
# Metadata Standard

Every destination page requires:

- Unique title
- Unique meta description
- Canonical URL
- Locale
- Alternate-language URL
- Open Graph title
- Open Graph description
- Open Graph image
- Last updated
- Last verified

## Suggested URL Pattern

```text
/id/bali/tanah-lot
/en/bali/tanah-lot
```

meta.title: Metadata Standard
meta.description: SEO metadata requirements for every destination page.

/docs/seo/schema.content:
# Schema Reference

Use structured data only when it matches visible page content.

Recommended types:

- `TouristAttraction`
- `Place`
- `GeoCoordinates`
- `BreadcrumbList`
- `FAQPage`
- `ImageObject`

Do not include hidden FAQ content in `FAQPage`.
